channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: PlayStation first-party game sales show a decline since 2020
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation first-party game sales have been largely in decline since 2020.

Via a report from Game File, each year since 2020, Sony has sold fewer copies of the games it has developed and/or published on PlayStation.

The biggest difference is between the fiscal years 2020 and 2024. In the former, Sony's first-party titles had 58.4 million sales. In the latter, the figure dropped to 28.9 million, less than half.

The decline has been steady during the last couple of years. FY21 saw 43.9 million sales, FY22 saw 43.5 million, and FY23 saw 39.7 million.

Numbers increased slightly in FY2025, totaling 32.1 million, which was the first upswing in years.

The context of some of these years is important. In the 12 months between the spring of 2020 and the winter of 2021, Sony released The Last of Us Part II and Ghost of Tsushima. It also launched the PlayStation 5.

As a reminder, The Last of Us Part II sold 10 million copies in its first two years. On the other hand, Ghost of Tsushima sold five million copies in its first four months and accrued 9.73 million sales by its two-year mark.

Related:Tekken 8 game director Kohei Ikeda has left Bandai Namco

During FY24, Sony released Astro Bot, which sold 1.5 million copies in its first nine weeks. Via Gematsu, Sony's financial reporting showed 37 percent of buyers had not purchased any first-party software other than Astro Bot.

It was also the year in which PlayStation rolled out Concord, the sci-fi shooter that was shut down two weeks after its launch. Following the game's closure, Sony shuttered its developer, Firewalk Studios.

Since then, Sony has been saying that it "learned a lot" from Concord's underperformance and expressed confidence that games like Marathon wouldn't repeat the same mistakes.

The company's most recent total from April 2025 through March 2026 showed the first big rebound in a half-decade. In the fiscal report for the third quarter ended December 31, 2025, Sony confirmed that Ghost of Yōtei exceeded expectations by outperforming its predecessor, Ghost of Tsushima, over the same period of time.

In the months since, Sony announced a price increase for PlayStation Plus, as well as its PlayStation consoles. Hardware sales are down amid said price hikes and the ongoing memory shortage problem. The company sold 1.5 PlayStation 5 consoles during Q4, marking a decline year-on-year compared with the 2.8 million consoles sales in the previous year.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/playstation-first-party-game-sales-are-in-decline
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
